content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
L. T. Buchanan, Jr., Chief 
Division of Administration 
Department of Motor Vehicles 
P.O. Box 11828 
Sacramento, 
CA 
95853-1101 
Dear Mr. Buchanan: 
July 23, 1986 
Re: 
FPPC No. A-86-200 
This is in reply to your letter dated June 12, 1986, 
concerning the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the 
Political Reform Act, and your letter dated June 20, 1986, in 
which you provided further information. 
In a subsequent telephone 
conversation, Mr. Jay Ellett of your office provided me with 
additional information. 
QUESTION 
You asked on behalf of Mr. Livingston, the Chief Deputy 
Director of the Department of Motor Vehicles, if he has incurred a 
reporting obligation with regard to free transportation which was 
provided to him by an Assemblyman. 
The facts surrounding receipt of the transportation are: 
the 
Chief Deputy Director of the Department of Motor Vehicles was 
provided with transportation in a private plane. 
An Assemblyman 
provided the transportation in a plane which is made available to 
the Assemblyman for his use. 
The trip from Sacramento to Los 
Angeles was for the purpose of participating in a press conference 
concering the Department of Motor Vehicles' recent Amnesty 
Program. 
The press conference was sponsored by the Assemblyman, 
and the Assemblyman requested that the Chief Deputy Director 
accompany him to Los Angeles to participate in the press 
conference. 
The Assemblyman would have made the trip to Los 
Angeles anyway in order to attend the press conference. 
ANSWER 
The Chief Deputy Director is not required to report receipt of 
the free transportation as a "gift" on his statement of Economic 
Interests. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 0 Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • (916) 322~566C 


L. T. Buchanan, Jr. 
July 23, 1986 
Page 2 
This answer is based on the analysis of a similar question 
contained in the Fair Political Practices commission opinion 
issued to Peter G. stone, No. 77-003 (3 FPPC Opinions 52 at 56). 
(A copy of the stone opinion is enclosed for your information.) 
Thank you for your inquiry. 
Please call me at (916) 322-5662 
if you have any additional questions. 
JP:kt 
Enclosure 
/)., 
/ 
sincerelY,!lz 
~~r:./Jt.(,;{L(~Z£'u!-c[ 
'/ Jeanne Pritchard 
Division Chief, Technical 
Asistance & Analysis Division 


OF MOTOR VEHICLES 
DIVISION OF ADMINISTRATION 
O. BOX 11828 
SACRAMENTO, CA 95853-1101 
June 12, 1986 
Fair political Practices commission 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
Attention: 
Ms. Betty Brown 
Dear Ms. Brown: 
JUN 17 9 28 AH '86 
During April of this year, your office was contacted for instructions on 
a specific incident which may need to be reported in the 1986-87 filing 
period. 
At that time, you were going to request a rulingi however, we 
have not yet received any further communication with your office. 
The requested information relates to free transportation furnished our 
Chief Deputy Director in a private plane owned by an Assemblyman. 
The 
purpose of the trip (Sacramento to Los Angeles) was to give a news 
release regarding our recent Amnesty Program. It is not certain if the 
commercial value of the trip should be reported as Income-Gifts (Schedule 
F) or if it is reportable as travel was within california. 
Please inform 
this office of your ruling as soon as possible. 
If you need further information, you may contact Joan Reid at 732-7643 • 
•• BUCHANAL~, JR., Chief 
bivitsion of Administration 
j cCi : 
A. Livingston, Chief Deputy Director, DMV 
f i 
j
